items.text:
PSYC 210: Introduction to Cognitive Psychology — Course Syllabus

Instructor: Dr. Marina Velasquez | Office: Harlow Hall, Room 318 | Office hours: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM

This course meets Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays from 10:00 AM to 10:50 AM in Lindgren Hall, Room 204. Students must bring the assigned readings to every session, as in-class discussion counts toward participation.

Grading is divided as follows: two midterm exams (20% each), a final research paper (35%), and weekly response posts (25%). The research paper is due December 8 and must be submitted through the course portal by 11:59 PM. Late papers lose five percentage points per day.

A lab fee of $20 covers access to the online experiment software and is payable at the registrar's office by September 18. Students who have not paid the fee by that date cannot access the software for the first graded assignment.

Make-up exams are granted only with written documentation. Please email the instructor at least 48 hours in advance to request accommodation.

questions.stem: According to the syllabus, what portion of the grade comes from the final research paper?

options.A: Twenty percent of the final grade
options.B: Thirty-five percent of the final grade
options.C: Twenty-five percent of the final grade
options.D: Fifty percent of the final grade

questions.explanation: The grading breakdown lists the final research paper as worth 35% of the grade.

questions.stem: What must students do to be eligible for a make-up exam?

options.A: Pay the lab fee at the registrar's office in advance
options.B: Attend the instructor's Tuesday afternoon office hours
options.C: Provide written documentation and email 48 hours ahead
options.D: Submit a response post through the course portal early

questions.explanation: The syllabus states make-up exams are granted only with written documentation and that students must email at least 48 hours in advance.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who has not paid the lab fee by September 18?

options.A: That student cannot complete the first graded assignment on time
options.B: That student must drop the course before the first midterm exam
options.C: That student loses five percentage points from the research paper
options.D: That student will be moved into a different section of the course

questions.explanation: The fee covers access to the experiment software, and students who have not paid by September 18 cannot access it for the first graded assignment, so they could not complete that assignment.

items.text:
Posted by Tomás R. in the Westbrook University Students group

Just got back from the Outdoor Club's weekend trip to Pinecrest Ridge and honestly it was one of the best things I've done all semester. I almost didn't sign up because I'd never been camping before, but the trip leaders made everyone feel welcome, even those of us who showed up with no gear at all.

The club lends out tents and sleeping bags for free, which I had no idea about. You just reserve them online a week ahead. The hike up to the lookout was tougher than I expected, but the view at the top was completely worth the climb.

If you're nervous about going alone, don't be. I drove up not knowing a single person and came back with a whole group of new friends. The next trip is in three weeks, and I've already signed up again. Highly recommend it to anyone who's been thinking about it but hasn't taken the leap yet.

questions.stem: According to the post, how can students obtain camping equipment for the trip?

options.A: They buy discounted gear at the club's weekend booth
options.B: They borrow it free by reserving online a week ahead
options.C: They share equipment with the assigned trip leaders
options.D: They rent tents from the university outdoor center

questions.explanation: The post says the club lends out tents and sleeping bags for free and that you reserve them online a week ahead.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this post?

options.A: To warn students about the difficulty of the hike up the ridge
options.B: To complain that the trip leaders did not provide enough gear
options.C: To encourage hesitant students to join the Outdoor Club trip
options.D: To organize a carpool for the upcoming Pinecrest Ridge outing

questions.explanation: The author shares a positive first experience and repeatedly urges nervous or undecided students to sign up, making encouragement the main purpose.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the author's attitude toward first-time participants?

options.A: He thinks experienced campers enjoy the trips far more
options.B: He believes beginners can feel comfortable and included
options.C: He recommends that newcomers bring their own equipment
options.D: He warns that going alone makes the trip less enjoyable

questions.explanation: The author, a first-timer with no gear who knew no one, describes being made to feel welcome and leaving with new friends, implying beginners can feel comfortable and included.
